members.name: GetOverlappingAreas
members.summary: Returns a list of intersecting [Area3D]s. The overlapping area's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_layer] must be part of this area's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_mask] in order to be detected. For performance reasons (collisions are all processed at the same time) this list is modified once during the physics step, not immediately after objects are moved. Consider using signals instead.

members.name: GetOverlappingBodies
members.summary: Returns a list of intersecting [PhysicsBody3D]s, [SoftBody3D]s, and [GridMap]s. The overlapping body's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_layer] must be part of this area's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_mask] in order to be detected. For performance reasons (collisions are all processed at the same time) this list is modified once during the physics step, not immediately after objects are moved. Consider using signals instead. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will not return any such bodies.

members.name: HasOverlappingAreas
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if intersecting any [Area3D]s, otherwise returns [code]false[/code]. The overlapping area's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_layer] must be part of this area's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_mask] in order to be detected. For performance reasons (collisions are all processed at the same time) the list of overlapping areas is modified once during the physics step, not immediately after objects are moved. Consider using signals instead.

members.name: HasOverlappingBodies
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if intersecting any [PhysicsBody3D]s, [SoftBody3D]s, or [GridMap]s, otherwise returns [code]false[/code]. The overlapping body's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_layer] must be part of this area's [member CollisionObject3D.collision_mask] in order to be detected. For performance reasons (collisions are all processed at the same time) the list of overlapping bodies is modified once during the physics step, not immediately after objects are moved. Consider using signals instead. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will not consider such bodies.

members.name: OverlapsArea
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the given [Area3D] intersects or overlaps this [Area3D], [code]false[/code] otherwise. [b]Note:[/b] The result of this test is not immediate after moving objects. For performance, list of overlaps is updated once per frame and before the physics step. Consider using signals instead.

members.name: OverlapsBody
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the given physics body intersects or overlaps this [Area3D], [code]false[/code] otherwise. [param body] argument can either be a [PhysicsBody3D], [SoftBody3D], or a [GridMap] instance. While GridMaps are not physics body themselves, they register their tiles with collision shapes as a virtual physics body. [b]Note:[/b] The result of this test is not immediate after moving objects. For performance, list of overlaps is updated once per frame and before the physics step. Consider using signals instead. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will return [code]false[/code] in such cases.

members.name: AngularDamp
members.summary: The rate at which objects stop spinning in this area. Represents the angular velocity lost per second. See [member ProjectSettings.physics/3d/default_angular_damp] for more details about damping.

members.name: AngularDampSpaceOverride
members.summary: Override mode for angular damping calculations within this area.

members.name: AudioBusName
members.summary: The name of the area's audio bus.

members.name: AudioBusOverride
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the area's audio bus overrides the default audio bus.

members.name: Gravity
members.summary: The area's gravity intensity (in meters per second squared). This value multiplies the gravity direction. This is useful to alter the force of gravity without altering its direction.

members.name: GravityDirection
members.summary: The area's gravity vector (not normalized).

members.name: GravityPoint
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], gravity is calculated from a point (set via [member gravity_point_center]). See also [member gravity_space_override].

members.name: GravityPointCenter
members.summary: If gravity is a point (see [member gravity_point]), this will be the point of attraction.

members.name: GravityPointUnitDistance
members.summary: The distance at which the gravity strength is equal to [member gravity]. For example, on a planet 100 meters in radius with a surface gravity of 4.0 m/s², set the [member gravity] to 4.0 and the unit distance to 100.0. The gravity will have falloff according to the inverse square law, so in the example, at 200 meters from the center the gravity will be 1.0 m/s² (twice the distance, 1/4th the gravity), at 50 meters it will be 16.0 m/s² (half the distance, 4x the gravity), and so on. The above is true only when the unit distance is a positive number. When this is set to 0.0, the gravity will be constant regardless of distance.

members.name: GravitySpaceOverride
members.summary: Override mode for gravity calculations within this area.

members.name: LinearDamp
members.summary: The rate at which objects stop moving in this area. Represents the linear velocity lost per second. See [member ProjectSettings.physics/3d/default_linear_damp] for more details about damping.

members.name: LinearDampSpaceOverride
members.summary: Override mode for linear damping calculations within this area.

members.name: Monitorable
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], other monitoring areas can detect this area.

members.name: Monitoring
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the area detects bodies or areas entering and exiting it.

members.name: Priority
members.summary: The area's priority. Higher priority areas are processed first. The [World3D]'s physics is always processed last, after all areas.

members.name: ReverbBusAmount
members.summary: The degree to which this area applies reverb to its associated audio. Ranges from [code]0[/code] to [code]1[/code] with [code]0.1[/code] precision.

members.name: ReverbBusEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the area applies reverb to its associated audio.

members.name: ReverbBusName
members.summary: The name of the reverb bus to use for this area's associated audio.

members.name: ReverbBusUniformity
members.summary: The degree to which this area's reverb is a uniform effect. Ranges from [code]0[/code] to [code]1[/code] with [code]0.1[/code] precision.

members.name: WindAttenuationFactor
members.summary: The exponential rate at which wind force decreases with distance from its origin. [b]Note:[/b] This wind force only applies to [SoftBody3D] nodes. Other physics bodies are currently not affected by wind.

members.name: WindForceMagnitude
members.summary: The magnitude of area-specific wind force. [b]Note:[/b] This wind force only applies to [SoftBody3D] nodes. Other physics bodies are currently not affected by wind.

members.name: WindSourcePath
members.summary: The [Node3D] which is used to specify the direction and origin of an area-specific wind force. The direction is opposite to the z-axis of the [Node3D]'s local transform, and its origin is the origin of the [Node3D]'s local transform. [b]Note:[/b] This wind force only applies to [SoftBody3D] nodes. Other physics bodies are currently not affected by wind.

signals.name: AreaEntered
signals.summary: Emitted when the received [param area] enters this area. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code].

signals.name: AreaExited
signals.summary: Emitted when the received [param area] exits this area. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code].

signals.name: AreaShapeEntered
signals.summary: Emitted when a [Shape3D] of the received [param area] enters a shape of this area. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code]. [param local_shape_index] and [param area_shape_index] contain indices of the interacting shapes from this area and the other area, respectively. [param area_rid] contains the [RID] of the other area. These values can be used with the [PhysicsServer3D]. [b]Example:[/b] Get the [CollisionShape3D] node from the shape index: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var other_shape_owner = area.shape_find_owner(area_shape_index) var other_shape_node = area.shape_owner_get_owner(other_shape_owner) var local_shape_owner = shape_find_owner(local_shape_index) var local_shape_node = shape_owner_get_owner(local_shape_owner) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

signals.name: AreaShapeExited
signals.summary: Emitted when a [Shape3D] of the received [param area] exits a shape of this area. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code]. See also [signal area_shape_entered].

signals.name: BodyEntered
signals.summary: Emitted when the received [param body] enters this area. [param body] can be a [PhysicsBody3D], [SoftBody3D] or [GridMap]. [GridMap]s are detected if their [MeshLibrary] has collision shapes configured. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will not emit this signal in such cases.

signals.name: BodyExited
signals.summary: Emitted when the received [param body] exits this area. [param body] can be a [PhysicsBody3D], [SoftBody3D] or [GridMap]. [GridMap]s are detected if their [MeshLibrary] has collision shapes configured. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will not emit this signal in such cases.

signals.name: BodyShapeEntered
signals.summary: Emitted when a [Shape3D] of the received [param body] enters a shape of this area. [param body] can be a [PhysicsBody3D], [SoftBody3D] or [GridMap]. [GridMap]s are detected if their [MeshLibrary] has collision shapes configured. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code]. [param local_shape_index] and [param body_shape_index] contain indices of the interacting shapes from this area and the interacting body, respectively. [param body_rid] contains the [RID] of the body. These values can be used with the [PhysicsServer3D]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will not emit this signal in such cases. [b]Example:[/b] Get the [CollisionShape3D] node from the shape index: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var body_shape_owner = body.shape_find_owner(body_shape_index) var body_shape_node = body.shape_owner_get_owner(body_shape_owner) var local_shape_owner = shape_find_owner(local_shape_index) var local_shape_node = shape_owner_get_owner(local_shape_owner) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

signals.name: BodyShapeExited
signals.summary: Emitted when a [Shape3D] of the received [param body] exits a shape of this area. [param body] can be a [PhysicsBody3D], [SoftBody3D] or [GridMap]. [GridMap]s are detected if their [MeshLibrary] has collision shapes configured. Requires [member monitoring] to be set to [code]true[/code]. See also [signal body_shape_entered]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot Physics does not support reporting overlaps with [SoftBody3D], so will not emit this signal in such cases.

values.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE
values.summary: This area adds its gravity/damping values to whatever has been calculated so far (in [member priority] order).

values.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE_REPLACE
values.summary: This area adds its gravity/damping values to whatever has been calculated so far (in [member priority] order), ignoring any lower priority areas.

values.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_DISABLED
values.summary: This area does not affect gravity/damping.

values.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE
values.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damping, even the defaults, ignoring any lower priority areas.

values.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE_COMBINE
values.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damping calculated so far (in [member priority] order), but keeps calculating the rest of the areas.

constants.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_DISABLED
constants.summary: This area does not affect gravity/damping.

constants.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE
constants.summary: This area adds its gravity/damping values to whatever has been calculated so far (in [member priority] order).

constants.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_COMBINE_REPLACE
constants.summary: This area adds its gravity/damping values to whatever has been calculated so far (in [member priority] order), ignoring any lower priority areas.

constants.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE
constants.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damping, even the defaults, ignoring any lower priority areas.

constants.name: SPACE_OVERRIDE_REPLACE_COMBINE
constants.summary: This area replaces any gravity/damping calculated so far (in [member priority] order), but keeps calculating the rest of the areas.
